Hello, my name is Minh and I am nineteen years old. I come from a small town in the north of Vietnam, but I now live in the city because I am a first-year student. I study business at university, and I hope to open my own coffee shop one day. In my free time, I love reading novels and playing badminton with my classmates. I am quite shy when I meet new people, but my friends say that I am kind and always ready to help. I usually spend my weekends with my family, and my mother often teaches me how to cook traditional dishes.

The Nguyen family lives in a small house near the city. Last year, they decided to make their home greener. First, they put solar panels on the roof to get free energy from the sun. Then they started growing vegetables in the back garden, so they buy less food from the market. Every week, the children sort the rubbish into different boxes for recycling. Their mother also fixed the old taps because they wasted a lot of water. At first, the changes were not easy, but now the family spends less money and feels proud. They believe that small actions at home can help protect the environment for everyone.

Linda is a nurse who lives in a small town. On weekdays, she wakes up at six o'clock and drinks a cup of tea before work. She rides her bicycle to the hospital because the roads are often busy in the morning. Her shift starts at seven and finishes at three in the afternoon. After work, she usually buys fresh vegetables at the market near her home and cooks dinner for her family. In the evening, Linda likes reading books or calling her sister who lives in another city. She goes to bed early because her job makes her very tired. On weekends, she relaxes in the garden and meets her friends.

The Green Valley Funfair opens every summer in our town, and it is one of the most popular events of the year. This year the funfair has more than twenty rides, including a giant wheel that gives visitors a beautiful view of the whole valley. Families with young children love the gentle carousel, while teenagers prefer the fast roller coaster near the entrance. In the evening, a small stage hosts free music shows, and food stalls sell snacks from many countries. Tickets are cheaper if you buy them online before the weekend. Last year, more than ten thousand people visited, and the organisers hope this summer will be even busier.

Last summer, Maria and her family decided to explore the north of Portugal by train instead of driving. They liked the idea of relaxing and watching the countryside pass by the window. Their first stop was Porto, a lively city famous for its old bridges and riverside cafés. They spent two days walking through narrow streets and tasting local food. From Porto, they took a slow regional train into the Douro Valley, where green hills are covered with vineyards. Maria's favourite moment was a short boat trip along the river at sunset. Although the journey took longer than a car, the family agreed that travelling by train was far more enjoyable and much less stressful.

Many people today spend hours sitting at a desk or looking at their phones. Doctors say this way of living can be bad for our health. When we sit for too long, our muscles become weak and we may put on weight. Sitting all day can also make us feel tired and stressed. The good news is that small changes can help a lot. Experts suggest standing up and walking around for a few minutes every hour. Drinking enough water and taking the stairs instead of the lift are also useful. Doing light exercise, such as stretching, keeps the body active. If we build these healthy habits into our daily routine, we will feel better and have more energy.

Many schools around the world now use technology to help students learn. In the past, teachers wrote everything on the blackboard, and students copied it into their notebooks. Today, many classrooms have computers, projectors, and fast internet. Students can watch videos, take online quizzes, and read digital books. Some students even study at home using their laptops. This is called online learning, and it has become very popular in recent years. Online learning is useful because students can study at any time and review lessons whenever they want. However, it also has problems. Some students feel lonely without their classmates, and others find it hard to focus at home. Most teachers believe that a mix of both methods works best.

s3_raw.text: Section 3 Questions 16-20 (5 points)
Read the text and questions below. For each question, circle the letter next to the correct answer (A, B, C or D).

Ha Long Bay is one of the most beautiful places in northern Vietnam. It is famous for its thousands of small limestone islands that rise out of the green water. Many of the islands have caves inside them, and visitors can explore these caves by boat. The best way to see the bay is to take a boat trip that lasts one or two days. During the trip, tourists can swim, go kayaking, and watch the sun set over the water. The weather is usually warm, but it can be foggy in winter. Because so many people visit every year, the local government works hard to keep the water clean and protect the natural beauty of the area.
16. What is Ha Long Bay most famous for?
A. Its tall mountains
B. Its thousands of limestone islands
C. Its large modern cities
D. Its long wide rivers
17. How can visitors explore the caves?
A. By car
B. On foot along a road
C. By boat
D. By plane
18. How long does the best boat trip last?
A. One or two hours
B. One or two days
C. A whole week
D. Half a day
19. What can the weather be like in winter, according to the passage?
A. Very hot
B. Snowy
C. Foggy
D. Windy and dry
20. Why does the local government work hard?
A. To build more hotels on the islands
B. To keep the water clean and protect the area
C. To stop tourists from visiting the bay
D. To make the small islands bigger

s4_raw.text: Section 4 Questions 21-30 (10 points)
Read the text below and fill each of the blanks with ONE suitable word from the box.
visit travelled beautiful walked see friendly ate took forget return arrived expensive bought quickly remember

Last summer, my family decided to (21) ...... a small town in the mountains. We (22) ...... there by train through green mountains and forests. The town was very (23) ...... and quiet, with old wooden houses along narrow streets. On the first day, we (24) ...... to the top of a hill to see an ancient temple. From there, we could (25) ...... the whole valley below us. The local people were very (26) ...... and showed us the best places to eat. In the evening, we (27) ...... delicious food at a small restaurant near the river. The next morning, we (28) ...... some photos of the beautiful bridge in the town centre. I will never (29) ...... this wonderful trip, and I hope to (30) ...... there again next year.

w1_raw.text: Section 1 (10 points)
Finish each of the following sentences so that it means the same as the sentence before it.
People built this bridge two hundred years ago.
This bridge ______
This is the tallest tower in the city.
No other tower in the city ______
The path was too steep for us to climb.
The path was so ______
I started living in this town five years ago.
I have ______
The journey to the island lasts three hours by boat.
It takes ______

w1_answers.1: This bridge was built two hundred years ago.
w1_answers.2: No other tower in the city is as tall as this one.
w1_answers.3: The path was so steep that we could not climb it.
w1_answers.4: I have lived in this town for five years.
w1_answers.5: It takes three hours to travel to the island by boat.

w2_raw.text: Section 2 (20 points)
You are Nam. This is part of a letter you have received from Alex, your English pen-friend.
You have a penfriend named Alex who lives in Canada.
Alex wants to know about a famous place in your country.
Write a letter of about 100 words. In your letter, tell Alex about the place and answer these two questions:
What can visitors see and do there, and why do you think Alex should visit it?
Now write a letter to this pen-friend on the answer sheet. You should write about 100 words.
--- The end ---

1.stem: Many students enjoy ...... new languages because it helps them make foreign friends.

2.stem: I ...... my old English teacher since I left secondary school.

3.stem: For me, English grammar is ...... than Vietnamese grammar, but I practise it every day.

s2_raw.text: Section 2 Questions 11-14 (4 points)
Look at the text in each question. What does it say? Circle the letter next to the correct explanation (A, B or C).

11. Free English speaking club every Friday afternoon in the school library. All levels welcome. No booking needed. A. You must book a place before joining the club. B. The club is open to learners of any level. C. The club meets on Friday mornings. 12. Please return your borrowed language books within two weeks. Late returns cost extra money each day. A. You can keep the books for as long as you like. B. You pay more money for each day a book is late. C. Language books cannot be borrowed here. 13. Quiet, please. Language exam in progress. Turn off your mobile phone before you enter the hall. A. You may use your phone quietly inside the hall. B. Switch off your phone before you go into the hall. C. The exam has already finished. 14. Download our vocabulary app and learn ten new English words every day. The first month is free. A. The app teaches ten new words each day. B. You must pay to use the app in the first month. C. The app only works in English-speaking countries.

s3_raw.text: Section 3 Questions 16-20 (5 points)
Read the text and questions below. For each question, circle the letter next to the correct answer (A, B, C or D).

Learning a foreign language is not always easy, but it can be very useful. Maria comes from Spain, and she started learning English when she was ten years old. At first, she found grammar boring and difficult. However, she loved watching English films and singing English songs. These activities helped her learn many new words without much effort. Today Maria works as a tour guide in Madrid. She meets visitors from many countries and speaks to them in English every day. She says that speaking a second language has changed her life. Now she wants to learn a third language, Italian, because she thinks it sounds beautiful.
16. When did Maria start learning English?
A. When she was ten years old.
B. When she became a tour guide.
C. When she moved to Madrid.
D. When she was a small baby.
17. At first, what did Maria think about grammar?
A. It was easy and fun.
B. It was boring and difficult.
C. It was beautiful.
D. It was useful for her job.
18. What helped Maria learn many new words easily?
A. Reading grammar books.
B. Watching films and singing songs.
C. Working as a tour guide.
D. Living in England.
19. What is Maria's job now?
A. She is an English teacher.
B. She is a singer.
C. She is a tour guide.
D. She is a film maker.
20. Why does Maria want to learn Italian?
A. Because she thinks it sounds beautiful.
B. Because she needs it for her job.
C. Because her family is Italian.
D. Because it is very easy to learn.

s4_raw.text: Section 4 Questions 21-30 (10 points)
Read the text below and fill each of the blanks with ONE suitable word from the box.
languages money communicate afraid skills easy practise talk slowly widely speaking books learn happy difficult

Language is one of the most important (21) ...... that humans have. We use it every day to (22) ...... with other people, to share our ideas and to ask for help. There are thousands of different (23) ...... in the world, and each one has its own words and rules. Some children grow up (24) ...... two languages at the same time because their parents come from different countries. Learning a new language can be (25) ......, but it is also fun. When you travel to another country, you can (26) ...... to local people and understand their culture better. Many people study English because it is (27) ...... used in business and science. To improve quickly, you should (28) ...... a little every day and not be (29) ...... of making mistakes. Mistakes help you (30) ...... and become more confident.

w1_raw.text: Section 1 (10 points)
Finish each of the following sentences so that it means the same as the sentence before it.
English is more difficult than French for me.
French ______
I started learning Japanese three years ago.
I have ______
She speaks English very well.
She is ______
Learning grammar is not as interesting as speaking.
Speaking ______
Nobody in my class can write Chinese better than Lan.

w1_answers.1: French is easier than English for me.
w1_answers.2: I have learned Japanese for three years.
w1_answers.3: She is very good at speaking English.
w1_answers.4: Speaking is more interesting than learning grammar.
w1_answers.5: Lan writes Chinese the best in my class.

s2_raw.text: Section 2 Questions 11-14 (4 points)
Look at the text in each question. What does it say? Circle the letter next to the correct explanation (A, B or C).

11. Storm warning: strong winds are expected this afternoon. Please close all windows and stay indoors. A. The storm has already passed and it is safe outside. B. People should remain inside because of the strong winds. C. All windows must be kept wide open during the storm. 12. Beach closed today because of high waves and a rough sea. Swimming is not allowed. A. Visitors can still swim if they are careful. B. The beach is open for swimming as usual. C. Nobody is permitted to swim here today. 13. School buses may run late this morning because of the heavy snow. Please wait at your usual stop. A. The snow might delay the school buses. B. The buses have been cancelled for the whole week. C. Students should find another way to get to school. 14. Free umbrellas are available at the front desk. Please return them when the rain stops. A. You must pay some money to borrow an umbrella. B. You can borrow an umbrella here at no cost. C. The umbrellas are only for members of staff.

s3_raw.text: Section 3 Questions 16-20 (5 points)
Read the text and questions below. For each question, circle the letter next to the correct answer (A, B, C or D).

Linda lives in a small town near the mountains, where the weather changes very quickly. In the morning, the sky is often clear and the sun is warm, so many people go outside to work in their gardens. However, by the afternoon, dark clouds sometimes appear and bring heavy rain. Because of this, Linda always carries a small umbrella in her bag. She also checks the weather forecast on her phone every day before she leaves home. Last winter, it was extremely cold and snow covered the streets for a week. The children were very happy because their school was closed, but the adults found it difficult to drive to work.
16. Where does Linda live?
A. In a big city by the sea
B. In a small town near the mountains
C. In a village in the desert
D. On a farm far from any town
17. What is the weather often like in the morning?
A. Clear and warm
B. Dark and rainy
C. Cold and snowy
D. Windy and foggy
18. Why does Linda always carry an umbrella?
A. Because it snows every morning
B. Because heavy rain sometimes comes in the afternoon
C. Because she does not like the warm sun
D. Because her school tells her to
19. What does Linda do every day before she leaves home?
A. She waters all the plants in her garden
B. She calls her friends on the phone
C. She checks the weather forecast on her phone
D. She buys a new umbrella at the shop
20. Why were the children happy last winter?
A. Because they were given new warm coats
B. Because the sun was warm every day
C. Because their school was closed
D. Because they were able to drive to work

s4_raw.text: Section 4 Questions 21-30 (10 points)
Read the text below and fill each of the blanks with ONE suitable word from the box.
lives shining friends stay weather carefully much forecast wear improve raining cold many sunny snow

The weather has a big effect on our daily (21) ....... When the sun is (22) ......, most people feel happy and full of energy. They go outside to walk, play sports, or meet (23) ....... On rainy days, however, many people prefer to (24) ...... at home and read a book. In some countries, the (25) ...... is hot all year round, while in others it changes with the seasons. Farmers watch the sky very (26) ...... because they need enough rain for their crops. If there is too (27) ...... rain, the plants may die. Many people listen to the weather (28) ...... every morning so they know what to (29) ...... before they go out. In general, good weather can really (30) ...... our mood.

w1_raw.text: Section 1 (10 points)
Finish each of the following sentences so that it means the same as the sentence before it.
It is raining heavily, so we cannot play football outside.
We cannot play football outside because ______
The weather was so cold that the lake froze completely.
It was such ______
I have never seen such a strong storm before.
This is the strongest storm ______
If you do not take an umbrella, you will get wet.
Take an umbrella, ______
The rain was very heavy, but they continued their journey.
Despite ______

w1_answers.1: We cannot play football outside because it is raining heavily.
w1_answers.2: It was such cold weather that the lake froze completely.
w1_answers.3: This is the strongest storm I have ever seen.
w1_answers.4: Take an umbrella, or you will get wet.
w1_answers.5: Despite the heavy rain, they continued their journey.
